data.title: Brent Crude Futures Near $103 Amid Bearish Technical Indicators
data.description: Brent crude futures were trading close to $103 at the time of reporting, following a surge of over 40% in the past month. According to NS3.AI, a 4-hour head and shoulders pattern, coupled with RSI divergence, suggests a potential weakening in momentum. The article notes that this bearish setup could diminish if prices rise above $104.37.

data.title: Crypto stocks opened lower across the board, with Coinbase (COIN) falling more than 3%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.76%, the S&P 500 fell 0.60%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.71%. The crypto sector collectively weakened, with Coinbase falling 3.71%, Strategy (MSTR) falling 3.18%, Solana falling 2.99%, Bitmine falling 2.49%, SharpLink falling 1.84%, and Circle falling 1.80%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Analysts: The shadow of Trump's "weekend action" reappears, intensifying market tensions.
data.description: Investinglive analyst Adam Button stated that the Iranian Revolutionary Guard today clarified the rules for passage through the Strait of Hormuz: any ships traveling to or from ports of countries "supporting or allied with the Zionist-American camp," regardless of their final destination or route, will be prohibited from passage. The emphasis on "port ownership" is crucial. Previously, the market had envisioned a scenario where crude oil could still flow through the Strait of Hormuz to "friendly countries" like India, thereby reducing the daily supply gap to 2-3 million barrels through supply restructuring. However, based on the current statement, the Iranian Revolutionary Guard appears to be preparing for a larger-scale conflict with other Gulf states, including the UAE and Saudi Arabia. Furthermore, weekend risks are gradually escalating in the market. Trump's statements are not always credible, and he has a history of launching military actions on weekends. Currently, the US is massing troops in the region, and the market is concerned that Trump might launch an attack on Kharg Island or other Iranian targets after today's close or early Saturday morning. Whether this will lead to an escalation of the conflict or a substantial blow to Iran remains highly uncertain. (Jinshi)

data.title: Grayscale transferred approximately 9,787 ETH and 446 BTC to Coinbase.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, Grayscale transferred approximately 9,787 ETH, worth about $19.46 million, and approximately 446 BTC, worth about $29.43 million, to Coinbase Prime in the past 10 minutes.

data.title: CoinDesk 20 Declines as Most Assets See Losses
data.description: The CoinDesk 20 index experienced a decline, trading at 1,912.59, marking a decrease of 2.4% or 47.98 points since 4 p.m. ET on Thursday. According to NS3.AI, nineteen out of the twenty assets in the index saw a downturn. Notably, Bitcoin Cash (BCH) was the only asset to rise, gaining 0.8%. In contrast, Aptos (APT) fell by 4.6%, and Aave (AAVE) decreased by 3.2%.

data.title: Grayscale's Chief Legal Officer Highlights Shift in Crypto Regulation
data.description: Grayscale's Chief Legal Officer, Craig Salm, highlighted a significant shift in the regulatory landscape for cryptocurrencies. Grayscale posted on X. Salm noted that the environment has transitioned from a negative stance characterized by enforcement to a more positive one focused on guidance and rule-making. He shared these insights during the Blockworks Digital Asset Summit.

data.title: Crypto Stocks Slide Pre-Market as Coinbase Leads Declines
data.description: Crypto-related stocks in the U.S. are trading lower in pre-market hours, reflecting continued caution across digital asset markets.Shares of Coinbase declined 2.31%, leading losses among major crypto proxies. MicroStrategy fell 1.06%, while other crypto-linked equities including SBET (-0.46%) and BMNR (-0.15%) also moved lower.The weakness comes as Bitcoin trades near key levels around $70,000, with market structure still driven by macroeconomic uncertainty and geopolitical developments.

data.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Threat Raises Oil, Crypto Market Risk as Iran Warns of “Severe Measures”
data.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has claimed that the Strait of Hormuz has been closed, warning that any vessels passing through the critical shipping route will face “severe measures,” according to Iranian media reports cited by Reuters.The IRGC statement reportedly prohibits all vessels linked to “hostile allies and supporters of the U.S. and Israel” from transiting the channel. However, there has been no independent confirmation of a full closure or enforcement at the time of reporting.The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world’s most important oil transit chokepoints, handling a significant portion of global crude exports. Any disruption could have immediate implications for energy markets, with oil prices likely to rise on supply concerns.

data.title: Japan Bond Yields Surge to Multi-Month Highs, Raising Global Liquidity Concerns
data.description: Key TakeawaysJapan’s 10-year government bond yield rises to 2.38%, highest since Jan. 20.30-year yield climbs to 3.71%, while 40-year reaches 3.925%.Rising yields signal tightening financial conditions and potential global spillover.Higher yields may pressure risk assets, including Bitcoin and the crypto market.Japanese government bond (JGB) yields climbed to multi-month highs across the curve, signaling tightening financial conditions and raising potential implications for global markets.The 10-year JGB yield rose to 2.38%, marking its highest level since Jan. 20. Longer-duration bonds also saw significant moves, with the 30-year yield reaching 3.71% and the 40-year yield rising to 3.925%.The rise in yields reflects ongoing adjustments in Japan’s monetary policy environment, as markets continue to price in reduced accommodation and higher long-term rates.Japan’s bond market plays a critical role in global liquidity conditions. Rising yields can influence capital flows, particularly as Japanese investors reassess overseas allocations and domestic returns become more attractive.For global risk assets, including cryptocurrencies, higher sovereign yields typically signal tighter financial conditions. This can reduce liquidity and increase pressure on assets such as Bitcoin, which have shown sensitivity to macro-driven capital flows.

data.title: The yield on 30-year U.S. Treasury bonds rose to 4.986%, a new high since September last year.
data.description: The yield on 30-year U.S. Treasury bonds rose to 4.986%, a new high since September of last year. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Alpha Focus: Community Engagement Update
data.description:
Binance Wallet announced on X that it is seeking community feedback through its Binance Alpha Focus initiative. The platform encourages users to share their thoughts and insights on the current developments and trends within the Binance ecosystem. This initiative aims to foster a collaborative environment where users can actively participate in discussions and contribute to the platform's growth.

The Binance Alpha Focus initiative is part of the company's broader strategy to engage with its community and gather valuable input from its users. By inviting feedback, Binance seeks to understand the needs and preferences of its user base, ensuring that future developments align with community expectations. This approach not only enhances user satisfaction but also strengthens the relationship between Binance and its community members.

Users are encouraged to share their opinions and suggestions, which will be considered in shaping the platform's future direction. This open dialogue is expected to lead to improvements in services and offerings, ultimately benefiting the entire Binance community. The initiative underscores Binance's commitment to transparency and user-centric development, reinforcing its position as a leading platform in the digital asset space.

data.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guard: Launched devastating drone attacks on Israeli bases and airports.
data.description: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard: Since last night, the Iranian military has launched devastating drone attacks on Israeli military support bases and transport centers in Tel Aviv, as well as military troop concentrations at Ben Gurion Airport. This wave of drone attacks was aimed at striking specialized Israeli forces and disrupting enemy ground and air preparedness and support facilities and systems. The attacks are expected to pose a sustained threat to enemy supply lines and directly impact Israel's rapid, secure, and large-scale transportation capabilities, ultimately affecting the regime's offensive operational capabilities. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Pharos Network has partnered with Circle to integrate USDC and CCTP into its mainnet.
data.description: Pharos Network, an institutional-grade, high-performance parallel Layer 1 public chain built specifically for real-world finance, has announced a partnership with Circle. Circle's mainnet will deploy USDC and the cross-chain transfer protocol CCTP. This integration aims to provide USD settlement capabilities for the RealFi market on Pharos and enable native cross-chain functionality with over 20 mainstream blockchains via CCTP. Pharos plans to use USDC as the core settlement asset for RWA assets, DeFi, and global payment scenarios, while also leveraging Pharos Network's recently launched $10 million ecosystem incubation program to accelerate ecosystem development.

data.title: Yi Lihua: The crypto market has been disappointing and lacks disruptive innovation over the past four years.
data.description: Yi Lihua stated in an article on the X platform that the current crypto market is in a bear market and the war is not over, lacking a significant rebound. The crypto sector's performance over the past four years has been disappointing, with crypto middle-class investors, retail investors, and VCs all suffering losses, while exchanges, market makers, and project teams have continuously withdrawn funds. Currently, market inflows mainly come from Wall Street ETFs, DAT, and a small number of enthusiasts, and there has been no disruptive innovation in the crypto space.

data.title: Twenty One Capital Becomes Second-Largest Public Bitcoin Holder With 43,514 BTC After MARA Sale
data.description: Twenty One Capital, led by Jack Mallers, has become the second-largest publicly disclosed corporate holder of Bitcoin, with total holdings reaching 43,514 BTC.The shift follows a reported Bitcoin sale by Marathon Digital Holdings (MARA), which reduced its relative position among public company holders.Despite the change in rankings, Strategy continues to dominate corporate Bitcoin holdings with a total of 762,099 BTC, maintaining a significant lead over other institutional players.

data.title: Crypto Market Faces Challenges Amid Bear Market and Ongoing Conflict
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market is currently experiencing a bear market with no significant rebound in sight. According to Odaily, Yi Lihua expressed on the X platform that the ongoing conflict has further compounded the market's struggles. Over the past four years, the performance in the crypto sector has been disappointing, with retail investors, crypto middle class, and venture capitalists facing losses. Meanwhile, exchanges, market makers, and project teams continue to extract funds.

At present, the primary inflow of capital into the market comes from Wall Street ETFs, DAT, and a small number of dedicated believers. The crypto industry has yet to witness any groundbreaking innovations.

data.title: $13 Billion in Bitcoin Options Set to Expire on Deribit, Raising Volatility Risks
data.description: Approximately $13 billion worth of Bitcoin options are set to expire today on Deribit, marking one of the largest expiry events in recent weeks and a potential catalyst for short-term market volatility.Options expiries of this scale often influence price action as traders adjust positions, hedge exposure, or unwind contracts ahead of settlement. The event is particularly significant given Bitcoin’s current positioning near the $70,000 level, a key psychological and technical support zone.

data.title: XRP Trades Near $1.36 Amid Bullish RSI Divergence
data.description: XRP is currently trading around $1.36, with a bullish Relative Strength Index (RSI) divergence emerging on the daily chart. According to NS3.AI, significant holders, often referred to as whales, have reduced their XRP holdings by 1.32 billion since early October 2025. For XRP to overcome its lower-high structure, it needs to reclaim the $1.60 level.

data.title: ECB Report Highlights DeFi Projects' Challenges with MiCA Exemptions
data.description:
According to NS3.AI, a report from the European Central Bank (ECB) has raised concerns about certain decentralized finance (DeFi) projects potentially not qualifying for exemptions under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation. The report highlights that more than half of the tokens in some DeFi projects are frequently held by protocol teams or exchanges, which may affect their eligibility for MiCA exemptions. Projects such as Aave, Sky, Uniswap, and Ampleforth were cited as examples in the document.

Additionally, the report points out issues with governance votes within these projects, noting that they often rely on delegated representatives. The identities of these representatives cannot be reliably linked to the actual token holders, posing challenges for transparency and accountability in governance processes.

data.title: Dollar May Strengthen Amid Iran Conflict and Market Volatility
data.description: The dollar could see further strengthening if the conflict in Iran leads to increased flows into safe-haven assets, according to a report by Mitsubishi UFJ analyst Derek Halpenny. According to ChainCatcher, Halpenny suggests that a significant downturn in the stock market could also bolster the dollar's strength. In a more severe scenario, if Brent crude oil prices rise from $110 per barrel to a range between $120 and $160, the dollar index might climb to around 105.
